meta.title: Recurrence after a First Unprovoked Seizure — by risk factor
meta.authors: Chen XY, Zhou FH, Tan G, et al.
meta.outcome: Cumulative risk of seizure recurrence after a first unprovoked seizure, by clinical risk factor (10-year prospective cohort, n=201)
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. Cumulative risk of seizure recurrence after a first unprovoked seizure, from a 10-year prospective cohort of 201 patients (Chen et al., Epilepsy Res 2024). Choose a clinical factor to see the recurrence curve for that group; the curves are group averages (single-factor), not a combined personal score.

Key findings

  • Overall recurrence: 29% at 6 months, 36% at 1 year, 41% at 2 years, ~58% at 10 years — most recurrences happen in the first 6–12 months.
  • Epileptiform discharges on EEG (~76%) and a prior brain insult (~76%) are the strongest, independent predictors (adjusted RR 2.5 and 2.1).
  • Nocturnal seizures (~71%), abnormal MRI (~65%) and focal onset also raise recurrence; combining epileptiform EEG + prior insult gives a ~7-fold risk.

Because most recurrences occur early, the chance of a seizure in the next year (COSY) is highest soon after the first seizure and falls with time seizure-free — relevant to driving decisions. Demonstration tool; each curve is an overall (unadjusted) Kaplan–Meier estimate.

model.selector_info: Pick a factor to display its recurrence curve after a first unprovoked seizure. Each is an overall single-factor Kaplan–Meier curve (not a combined per-patient score). Endpoints are the paper's Table-1 cumulative recurrence rates; the timeline follows the cohort's overall recurrence pattern (most recurrences early).

Overall (any first unprovoked seizure).info: Cohort-wide cumulative recurrence after a first unprovoked seizure: 29% at 6 mo, 36% at 1 yr, 41% at 2 yr, 48% at 5 yr, ~58% at 10 yr. Most recurrences happen early.

EEG: normal.info: Normal video-EEG — the lowest-risk EEG group. ~45% cumulative recurrence at 10 yr.

EEG: non-epileptiform abnormal.info: Non-epileptiform abnormalities on video-EEG. ~52% cumulative recurrence.

EEG: epileptiform discharges.info: Epileptiform discharges on video-EEG — the strongest predictor (adjusted RR 2.5). ~76% cumulative recurrence, most within the first year.

Nocturnal seizure: no.info: Index seizure while awake. ~48% cumulative recurrence.

Nocturnal seizure: yes.info: Index seizure during sleep (nocturnal) — higher recurrence (~71%), rising early.

Prior brain insult: no.info: No history of prior brain injury/insult. ~55% cumulative recurrence.

Prior brain insult: yes.info: History of a prior brain insult (remote symptomatic) — independent predictor (adjusted RR 2.1). ~76% cumulative recurrence.

provenance.source: Chen 2024 Epilepsy Res Table 1 (endpoints) + Fig 2/3 (timeline) — PMID39388981, doi 10.1016/j.eplepsyres.2024.107457
provenance.notes: Selector-curve: per-factor endpoints from Table 1 cumulative recurrence rates; monthly shape from the overall KM timeline (Fig 2: 29.4/35.8/41.1/47.9/57.5% at 6/12/24/60/120mo), with a steeper early rise for high-risk groups (Fig 3). Single-factor (unadjusted) curves — not a combined model. Extracted 2026-07-07; confirm against Fig 3 before deploy.
